summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. Economic conditions remain sensitive to fuel and traded-goods costs: ONS reports CPI inflation at 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, and Brent crude prices remain in the low-$100s per barrel (recently settling around $105/bbl), reinforcing near-term exposure to renewed inflation pressure and transport-cost volatility.

Security and resilience pressures also remain Elevated. The Russia–Ukraine war continues to generate escalation and supply-route risk for Europe (with recent strikes reported in Odesa). Domestically, cyber risk remains elevated with the NCSC urging immediate action on newly disclosed Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities; patching and configuration checks are time-sensitive given how widely these edge appliances are deployed. UK natural-hazard risk is comparatively contained at present, with no active Met Office weather warnings and only a small number of Environment Agency flood alerts.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Economic risk remains Elevated: inflation is still above target and sensitive to fuel costs. ONS reports CPI inflation at 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, and Brent crude remains around the low-$100s per barrel, keeping upside risk to transport and input costs.
drivers: ONS: CPI inflation 3.3% (12 months to March 2026). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/latest
Reuters: Brent crude futures settled at $105.33 a barrel (Apr 24, 2026). https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-concerns-over-escalating-military-tensions-middle-east-2026-04-24/

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained
OBR (March 2026 EFO): real GDP growth forecast 1.1% in 2026 (0.3pp lower than November). https://obr.uk/efo/economic-and-fiscal-outlook-march-2026/

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security remains Elevated due to ongoing Russia–Ukraine conflict activity and associated escalation/spillover risk; UK domestic terrorism threat level remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters: Russian drone attack overnight on Odesa killed two and wounded more than a dozen (Apr 24, 2026). https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russian-attack-ukraines-odesa-kills-married-couple-officials-say-2026-04-24/
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No acute UK governance instability signal identified in reviewed official sources; institutions appear to be functioning normally.
drivers: GOV.UK (31 Mar 2026): press release notes the PM chaired a COBR(M) meeting on the situation being assessed; no new emergency governance measures identified in reviewed sources today. https://www.gov.uk/government/news/new-measures-coming-in-to-ease-cost-of-living-pressure-1-april-2026

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Near-term natural-hazard risk is Guarded: Met Office shows no active UK weather warnings, and Environment Agency flood risk is limited to a small number of flood alerts.
drivers: Met Office: UK weather warnings page shows no active warnings (viewed 26 Apr 2026). https://weather.metoffice.gov.uk/warnings-and-advice/uk-warnings
Environment Agency (GOV.UK): flood alerts and warnings page shows 2 flood alerts (viewed 26 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Social disruption risk remains Elevated due to ongoing London Underground industrial action with additional scheduled strike dates in May and June.
drivers: BBC: London Underground RMT strike action includes further 24-hour walkouts scheduled in May and June (published Apr 24, 2026). https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj94x99yex3o

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains Elevated due to urgent patching and configuration checks for newly disclosed Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities highlighted by the NCSC.
drivers: NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368 (Apr 22, 2026). https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Reuters: Brent crude futures settled at $105.33 a barrel (Apr 24, 2026). https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-rises-concerns-over-escalating-military-tensions-middle-east-2026-04-24/

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Mar 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI inflation was 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/latest

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: England flood alerts (headline count)
indicators.note: Environment Agency flood alerts and warnings page shows 2 flood alerts (viewed 26 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 26 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Brent crude indicator refreshed using Reuters settlement price ($105.33/bbl on Apr 24, 2026) rather than the Trading Economics snapshot.
Natural Hazards view re-checked: Met Office still shows no active UK weather warnings; Environment Agency still shows 2 flood alerts.
Political/Governance drivers refreshed with a relevant GOV.UK press release reference to a recent COBR(M) meeting (no new emergency measures identified in reviewed sources).
priority_watch_items: Fuel/inflation sensitivity: monitor whether sustained Brent strength feeds through into UK pump prices and near-term inflation expectations.
London transport disruption: monitor impacts as industrial action continues and as further strike dates in May/June approach.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation progress for Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway and monitor for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further Russia–Ukraine escalation affecting Black Sea-linked logistics and energy systems.
Flooding: monitor Environment Agency updates for any rapid escalation in local warnings/alerts following rainfall.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, Met Office, Environment Agency) with Reuters used for conflict and energy-market context and the BBC for transport disruption reporting.

The Environment Agency site uses a cookie banner and automated text extraction may omit headline alert counts; today's flood-alert count was verified via a page view on 26 Apr 2026. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings
